theme: tapping a cast bell to find any hidden crack
title: Bell-Sounding: Worst-Case Crack Probes
innovation_hook: Detection power for a single defect is the leading eigenvalue of that defect's DtN (boundary response) perturbation operator restricted to the zero-sum current subspace; because the score is a MIN over the whole defect family of the BEST probe for that defect, choosing q probes under budget is a spectral covering problem, not a per-defect optimization -- neither fact is visible from the circuit diagram alone.
strategies: trivial: reproduces the checker's own reference construction exactly -- a tight flanking (straddle) dipole around each of the first q listed defects, cycling through the list if q>m. Looks locally sensitive but barely pushes current past the rim, so it never really reaches the defect layer.

greedy: the obvious multimeter recipe -- for each of the first q listed defects, fire a spread (diametrically opposite) two-electrode dipole straight through its rim position. No worst-case reasoning about which defects go uncovered when q<m, and never more than 2 non-zero probe entries, so it cannot jointly focus AND diversify.

strong: for each defect, compute the leading eigenvector of that defect's DtN-perturbation quadratic form restricted to the zero-sum subspace (round to the integer/magnitude-bounded feasible set, falling back to the spread dipole only if it scores no worse), then greedily aim probes at whichever defect is currently worst-covered -- this both focuses current at the right depth AND spectrally covers the family within the probe budget.
invalid: emits a current pattern that both exceeds the per-electrode magnitude bound and fails current conservation (sum != 0) -- must be rejected, Ratio: 0.0.
